title: HTTP server layout cribs burrow's server module
rationale: Operator can flip between the two codebases without retraining: same Route/RouteContext/ServeHandle surface, same auth seam, same error envelope. The thin glue style (handlers wrap runs/registry/projects modules 1:1) keeps the wire layer small enough to read end-to-end.

content: Only /healthz is auth-exempt. /readyz is auth-required because its body reveals which checks failed (sensitive in a misconfigured deploy). AUTH_EXEMPT_PATHS lives in handlers.ts as the single source of truth.

Static UI catch-all registers AFTER API routes; the UI handler explicitly refuses API prefixes (/agents, /projects, /runs, /healthz, /readyz) so an API path with a typo returns a 404 envelope instead of accidentally rendering the SPA shell. Other GETs fall through to index.html so React Router handles deep links.

name: BridgeRegistry per-server lifecycle
description: Per-server registry owns live bridgeRunStream controllers. POST /runs registers a bridge via deps.bridges.start(); bootBridges resumes bridges for in-flight (queued|running) runs with burrow_run_id at startup; handle.stop() aborts everyone via stopAll() and awaits their drain. Resolved bridges auto-detach in their .finally so the registry stays small. Tests inject a stub bridge factory to avoid a real burrow.

title: WarrenError status mapping table; BurrowError passes through
rationale: Each WarrenError subclass maps to a fixed status in renderError: NotFoundError=404, ValidationError=400, StateTransitionError=409, BurrowUnreachableError=503, CanopyUnavailableError=503, ProjectUnavailableError=503, AgentSchemaError=422, RunSpawnError=500. BurrowError (rehydrated from burrow's HTTP envelope) forwards with its code+hint preserved so the same {code,message,hint} consumer works against warren and burrow without a second decoder.

content: Warren server env vars are warren-namespaced: WARREN_API_TOKEN (bearer; required unless --no-auth), WARREN_BIND_HOST/WARREN_BIND_PORT (TCP, default 0.0.0.0:8080), WARREN_BIND_SOCKET (unix; wins over host/port), WARREN_DATA_DIR (default /data), WARREN_DB_PATH (default <DATA_DIR>/warren.db), WARREN_UI_DIST_DIR (default <cwd>/src/ui/dist), WARREN_DISABLE_UI=1|true (no static serving), WARREN_LOG_LEVEL (pino level, default info).

registry/projects SpawnFn shape is (cmd: readonly string[], opts: { cwd: string; timeoutMs?: number }) => Promise<{ stdout: string; stderr: string; exitCode: number }> — non-zero exit codes are part of the result, not an exception. The default impl in handlers.ts uses Bun.spawn (with a setTimeout-based kill on timeoutMs) rather than execFile because execFile throws on non-zero, which doesn't match the SpawnResult contract.

name: ServerDeps wiring for tests
description: ServerDeps bundles repos + burrowClient + broker + bridges + canopyConfig + projectsConfig + logger + uiDistDir + optional now(). startServer takes ServerDeps so tests can spin up the wire layer without a real burrow socket: pass a stub fetch into BurrowClient, a stub bridge factory into createBridgeRegistry, and uiDistDir=null to skip static serving. main.ts owns the prod wiring; tests assemble deps inline.

title: POST /runs registers a bridge synchronously after spawnRun
rationale: Without deps.bridges.start(result.run.id, result.burrowRun.id) inside the createRun handler, the dispatched run emits events into burrow but warren's bridge never subscribes, so /runs/:id/events streams nothing. The bridge MUST be started in the same turn as the spawnRun success — there is no other surface where this happens. boot-time recoverActiveRunStreams covers the post-restart case, but never the post-spawn case.

title: Shared src/diagnostics/checks.ts backs /readyz and warren doctor
rationale: Phase 13 (warren-a29f) needed both /readyz and warren doctor to probe burrow + canopy clone existence + canopy clone cleanliness + bwrap availability. Duplicating the checks across the HTTP handler and the CLI command would have let the two surfaces drift. checks.ts exports pure functions (checkBwrap, checkCanopyClone, checkCanopyClean, checkBurrowReachable) returning {name, ok, message?, hint?} that doctor renders as NDJSON and /readyz packs into a single envelope. New readiness probes should land here, not in handlers.ts or doctor.ts.

content: ServerDeps.spawn is the spawn seam /readyz uses for the Phase-13 bwrap + canopy_clean probes. main.ts wires the production Bun.spawn adaptor (a third copy of defaultSpawn alongside handlers.ts and cli/output.ts; deliberate per existing layering — neither surface imports the other). Tests pass an okSpawn stub that returns exit 0 for bwrap --version and empty stdout for git status --porcelain.
